title: Psychology Foundations
description: This course introduces **the scientific study of mind and behavior**. Students learn the major branches of psychology, research methods, biological foundations, and the core concepts that underpin the field.

title: Cognitive Psychology
description: This course studies **how the mind acquires, processes, stores, and uses information**. It explores perception, attention, memory, language, reasoning, problem solving, and decision making.

title: Biological Psychology
description: This course studies **the biological basis of behavior**, exploring how the brain, nervous system, hormones, and genetics influence thoughts, emotions, learning, and behavior.

title: Developmental Psychology
description: This course studies **how people grow and change throughout the lifespan**, from conception to old age. It explores physical, cognitive, emotional, and social development.

title: Social Psychology
description: This course studies **how people's thoughts, feelings, and behaviors are influenced by the presence of others**. It explores social perception, attitudes, relationships, groups, and social influence.

title: Personality Psychology
description: This course studies **personality**—the enduring patterns of thoughts, emotions, and behaviors that make individuals unique. It explores major theories of personality, personality assessment, development, and applications.

title: Learning & Behavior
description: This course studies **how humans and animals acquire, modify, and maintain behavior** through experience. It covers the major theories of learning and their practical applications in education, therapy, and everyday life.

title: Abnormal Psychology
description: This course studies **psychological disorders**, including their causes, symptoms, diagnosis, and treatment. It examines mental health through biological, psychological, and social perspectives.

title: Clinical Psychology
description: This course studies **the assessment, diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of psychological disorders**. It focuses on evidence-based clinical practice and therapeutic approaches.

title: Industrial & Organizational Psychology
description: This course studies **how psychology is applied in workplaces and organizations**. It explores employee behavior, leadership, teamwork, motivation, organizational culture, and workplace well-being.

title: Research Methods & Statistics
description: This course studies **how psychological research is designed, conducted, analyzed, and interpreted**. It provides the scientific foundation for evaluating evidence and conducting psychological research.

title: Positive Psychology
description: This course studies **the science of human flourishing**, focusing on the strengths, habits, relationships, and experiences that contribute to happiness, resilience, meaning, and well-being.
